scenario_results.reason: Returned a customs-specific review summary tying HTSUS and valuation criteria to the supplied evidence bundle.
Missing product and valuation records produced a failed or unclear assessment with explicit remediation requirements.
Formal ruling authority was surfaced as controlling and changed the finding without unsupported interpolation.
Final signoff request was refused and routed to the correct owner with boundary-safe language.
Undervaluation and suspicious transaction indicators triggered fraud and legal escalation instead of completion.
Reconciliation and ledger conflict produced an escalation packet and prevented unsupported closure.
Missing tenant prerequisites returned an explicit low-confidence note and stop condition.
Public-source and tenant-policy conflict was escalated under tenant precedence rather than synthesized away.
Industry terms such as GRI, assists, and deductive value were normalized into the lane's evidence model.
Adjacent-lane work was rejected and routed to legal, transfer-pricing, or admissibility owners as appropriate.
Open findings and corrective evidence requirements were tracked through follow-up without claiming closure.
Tenant-specific stricter approval gates were honored while preserving the public customs baseline.

notes: Scenario set covers normal customs review, missing evidence, formal-authority changes, signoff refusal, fraud escalation, reconciliation conflict, and remediation tracking.
All required shared, review-assessment-and-inspection, and financial-services-and-insurance abilities are mapped to passing scenarios.
Runtime remains `specification-based` and this package does not claim a trusted built implementation.
